title: Gothofreda coriacea
intro: Gothofreda coriacea, also known as Wild Pear or Goat's Bread, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region and parts of China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and anti-bacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gothofreda coriacea include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion and diarrhea.
taxonomy: Gothofreda coriacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Gothofreda and is a type of orchid. Further classification includes the order Asparagales and class Liliopsida, which places it within the monocot group of plants. Gothofreda coriacea is native to Central and South America, where it grows in tropical and subtropical regions. The exact ranking of the species within the family Orchidaceae is still being studied and refined by botanists.
